sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=28, col=10), MOUSE(row=10, col=52).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 66, level 1.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'charcoal_left_green_node10': 'Decrements green by one state (23→14→5) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'charcoal_right_green_node12': 'Increments green by one state (5→14→23) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'charcoal_top_yellow_node19': 'Decrements yellow 14→5 (VERIFIED step 35)', 'charcoal_bottom_yellow_node22': 'Increments yellow by one state (5→14→23) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'dest_N_shape': 'Toggles green between adjacent states (VERIFIED steps 3-11)', 'dest_N_frame': 'Sets green to 23 (VERIFIED step 12)', 'dest_Y_shape': 'Toggles yellow between adjacent states (VERIFIED steps 5-6, 42)', 'red_dot': 'No gameplay effect (VERIFIED step 1)', 'red_cross': 'Static decoration, no effect (VERIFIED step 2)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'B': 'black background', 'G': 'dark gray (container frames, separators)', 'g': 'gray (outer container frames)', 'c': 'charcoal (inner boxes in destination containers - these are the real controls)', 'N': 'light green shapes (source node 2, dest nodes 13/14)', 'Y': 'yellow shapes (source node 16, dest nodes 20/23)', 'r': 'red dots: trackers in source containers (nodes 4,17) and red cross decorations (nodes 3,5,7,8,24,25,27,28)'}
- HUD map: {'row_63': 'gray/charcoal step counter bar, decreases by 1 pixel per action'}
- Win pattern: Unknown - tested states: (5,5), (14,5), (5,14), (23,14), (23,23), (5,23), (14,23), (14,14). None completed the level.
- Cross-level notes: Charcoal boxes in destination containers are increment/decrement controls for source shapes. Each source has 3 states: 5/14/23px.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 65 actions; a strong score needs about 20 or fewer. You are 3.2x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Node 17 (red dot) and node 6 (which turned out to be B/black, not g as I thought) both had no effect. I've now tested almost every object on the board.
Let me reconsider the problem completely. I've tested:
1. All charcoal boxes (increment/decrement)
2. All dest shapes (toggle state)
3. Both dest frames (green sets to 23, yellow no effect)
4. Both source frames (no effect)
5. Both separator bars (no effect)
6. Red dots (no effect)
7. Red crosses (no effect)
8. Black background (no effect)
And I've tested all 9 state combinations without completing the level.
What am I missing? Let me think about this differently.
What if the goal is not about the state combination, but about the ORDER of clicks? Like, I need to click on the charcoal boxes in a specific sequence?
Or what if the goal is to click on the charcoal boxes until they change color?
Or what if the goal is to click on the dest shapes until they change?
Wait, let me reconsider. I know that:
- Clicking on charcoal boxes changes the source shape state
- Clicking on dest shapes also changes the source shape state
But what if clicking on the charcoal boxes also changes the charcoal box itself? Like, the charcoal box might change color or size?
Let me check if the charcoal boxes change when I click on them. I've been focusing on the source shape changes, but maybe the charcoal boxes also change?
Actually, looking at my earlier tests, I only checked if the source shape pixel count changed. I didn't check if the charcoal boxes themselves changed.
Let me try clicking on a charcoal box and checking if the charcoal box itself changes (color, size, hash, etc.).
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the problem from a completely different angle. What if the goal is to make the source shapes match the dest shapes in some way? The dest shapes are 7px U-shapes. The source shapes are 5, 14, or 23px shapes.
But the source shapes can't be 7px, so they can't match the dest shapes in pixel count.
What if the goal is to make the source shapes disappear? Or to move them to the dest containers?
Actually, let me try something completely different. What if I need to click on the source shapes (nodes 2 and 16) directly? I know clicking on dest shapes toggles the source state, but what if clicking on the source shapes themselves does something different?
Let me try clicking on the green source shape (node 2) and the yellow source shape (node 16).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Almost all objects tested. Let me try clicking on the source shapes themselves.
seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Node 2: green source shape (N)
# Node 16: yellow source shape (Y)
for nid in [2, 16]:
    n = [x for x in seg['nodes'] if x['id'] == nid][0]
    b = n['boundary']
    r = sum(p[0] for p in b) // len(b)
    c = sum(p[1] for p in b) // len(b)
    print(f"Node {nid} ({n['color']}, {n['pixels']}px): clicking at ({r}, {c})")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])
    print(f"  Result: game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
    
    if last_transition and last_transition.before_frame:
        before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
        after = current_frame.segmentation
        before_dict = {n['id']: (n['color'], n['pixels'], n['hash']) for n in before['nodes']}
        after_dict = {n['id']: (n['color'], n['pixels'], n['hash']) for n in after['nodes']}
        all_ids = set(list(before_dict.keys()) + list(after_dict.keys()))
        diffs = []
        for iid in sorted(all_ids):
            bv = before_dict.get(iid, 'MISSING')
            av = after_dict.get(iid, 'MISSING')
            if bv != av and iid not in [29, 30]:
                diffs.append(f"id={iid}: {bv} -> {av}")
        if diffs:
            print(f"  Changes: {diffs}")
        else:
            print("  No gameplay changes")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"charcoal_boxes": "Increment/decrement source shapes", "dest_shapes": "Toggle source state", "green_dest_frame": "Sets green to 23", "all_other_objects": "No effect"}, "win_pattern": "Unknown - all 9 state combos failed, almost all objects tested", "cross_level_notes": "Trying source shapes directly", "level_log": "Level 1: 66 actions"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "green=5, yellow=5", "goal_model": "Trying source shapes directly", "recent_findings": ["Red dot and black background had no effect"], "open_questions": ["What do source shapes do when clicked?"], "current_plan": "Click source shapes (nodes 2 and 16)", "strategy_log": ["Testing remaining objects"], "failed_probes": ["All tested objects so far"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Node 2 (N, 5px): clicking at (10, 28)
  Result: game_over=False, level_completed=False
  No gameplay changes
Node 16 (Y, 5px): clicking at (28, 10)
  Result: game_over=False, level_completed=False
  No gameplay changes
